explanations.3: declare(strict_types=1) tells PHP to be strict about data types in function arguments and return values, so passing a string when a number is expected will cause an error instead of silent conversion.
explanations.13: php82: true means this configuration applies Rector rules for PHP version 8.2, which will update your code to use modern PHP 8.2 syntax and features.
explanations.14: The withPreparedSets call enables three pre-made bundles of code improvement rules: codeQuality checks for better patterns, deadCode removes unused code, and typeDeclarations adds type hints to functions.
